normalized_title: City of Melville Local Planning Policy 1.17 - Additional Development Exemptions
scope_summary: Lists additional categories of development that are exempt from requiring development approval in the City of Melville, made under clause 61(1) item 20 of Schedule 2 of the Planning and Development (Local Planning Schemes) Regulations 2015. Covers patios that do not meet R-Codes deemed-to-comply lot boundary setbacks, demolition of non-heritage buildings, dividing fences behind the street setback line, minor works to non-residential buildings (roofing, painting, cladding), and external fixtures not on the front elevation. Exemption criteria operate in addition to the standard clause 61 exemptions.

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: patios not meeting R-Codes deemed-to-comply lot boundary setbacks (exemption criterion)

key_numeric_standards.topic: patio coverage of outdoor living area
key_numeric_standards.unit: maximum proportion of primary outdoor living area
m (may be less if compliant with LPP 3.1 boundary wall length provisions)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: patios including roofs, gutters and eaves

key_numeric_standards.topic: patio post dimension not treated as boundary wall

key_numeric_standards.unit: m measured from approved natural ground level on the higher property
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: dividing fences behind the street setback line (exemption criterion, requires neighbour agreement)

cross_references.cited: Planning and Development (Local Planning Schemes) Regulations 2015
State Planning Policy 3.1 - Residential Design Codes
Local Planning Policy LPP 3.1 Residential Development

quality_flags: patio criteria list in Table 1 ends with a dangling 'and' before a page break, suggesting a final bullet may have been lost or merged during table extraction
policy cites the R-Codes by their former designation 'State Planning Policy 3.1' (now SPP 7.3)
cites Heritage of Western Australia Act 1990, which has been replaced by the Heritage Act 2018 (LEG-007)
